title: Congratulations!
text: Congratulations! You've just wrapped up the Graph Data Science in Practice workshop! In just four hours, you've gone from learning the basics to executing sophisticated graph analytics on a large scale. That's quite an achievement!

title: Module 1: GDS Foundations
text: In Module 1, you mastered the fundamentals of Graph Data Science. You learned about the GDS Project to Run to Write workflow, which is essential for setting up a graph data science project, executing algorithms, and writing results back to the database. [IMAGE: img-4-aura-graph-analytics-5-workshop-complete-s690-000-new] As you can see in this process flow diagram, it clearly illustrates the sequence of steps involved. You also explored how to create graph projections tailored to different analytical questions, understood various algorithm categories and when to use each, and got familiar with execution modes like stream, stats, mutate, and write.

title: Module 2: Community Detection for Fraud
text: In Module 2, you applied algorithms to tackle real-world fraud detection challenges. You used the Louvain algorithm to identify densely connected fraud networks and applied WCC for deterministic community assignment. [IMAGE: img-4-aura-graph-analytics-5-workshop-complete-s691-001-new] This structural diagram shows how Louvain and WCC algorithms work together to detect fraud within a network. You also filtered out noise using Degree Centrality and impressively reduced the search space by 98%, uncovering hidden fraud rings in the process.

title: Module 3: Python GDS Client
text: In Module 3, you built workflows that are ready for production. You connected to Neo4j and ran algorithms programmatically, analyzed citation networks using PageRank and Betweenness Centrality, detected research communities with Louvain, and generated FastRP embeddings for machine learning pipelines. These skills are crucial for integrating graph data science into real-world applications.

title: Module 4: Aura Graph Analytics
text: In Module 4, you scaled your analytics to the cloud. You learned to create ephemeral GDS Sessions for handling scalable workloads and optimized routes using Dijkstra's shortest path algorithm. [IMAGE: img-4-aura-graph-analytics-5-workshop-complete-s693-003-new] This diagram illustrates how ephemeral GDS Sessions and route optimization work together, showing nodes as locations and paths optimized for the shortest routes. You also explored finding alternative paths with Yen's k-shortest paths and learned how to run analytics without impacting production databases.

title: You're Now Ready To
text: You're now ready to apply GDS to your own datasets using the skills you've practiced. You can choose the right algorithms for different business problems, build production workflows with the Python GDS client, scale analytics with Aura Graph Analytics, and identify fraud patterns, optimize logistics, analyze networks, and much more.

title: Your Next Steps
text: Ready to dive deeper? Continue your learning journey with courses like Graph Data Science Fundamentals for comprehensive coverage of all GDS concepts, or Applied Algorithms in GDS for industry use cases with deep algorithm coverage. You can also enhance related skills with courses on Cypher Fundamentals and Graph Data Modeling Fundamentals. Don't forget to explore resources like the GDS Documentation for a complete algorithm reference, the Neo4j Community Forum to connect with other practitioners, and the GDS Developer Guide for practical examples.

title: Keep Practicing
text: Keep practicing by creating a free Neo4j AuraDB instance or downloading Neo4j Desktop. This will allow you to continue applying these skills to your own data and further solidify your understanding.

title: Thank You!
text: Thank you for completing the Graph Data Science in Practice workshop. We hope you enjoyed the hands-on experience and now feel confident in applying graph algorithms to solve real-world problems. Now, go build something amazing with graph data science!

title: Summary
text: You've successfully completed all 27 lessons across 4 modules. This includes 8 lessons on GDS foundations and workflow, 5 lessons on community detection for fraud, 9 lessons on Python GDS client workflows, and 5 lessons on Aura Graph Analytics and pathfinding. You're now equipped to apply graph algorithms to real business problems, build production workflows, and scale analytics with confidence.
